message:
fix(update): correct sed regex in jq-less bin_dir parser; cover with bats test

`update_preferred_user_bin_dir` falls back to a sed-based parse of
state.json when jq is not on PATH.  The capture group was
`\(^["]*\)` — a literal `^` followed by `["]*` — which never
matches the JSON value content (`^` is only an anchor at the start
of an alternative branch, and inside `\(...\)` it's just a literal
caret).  As a result the jq-less branch never returned a bin_dir,
so any system without jq fell through to the $HOME/.local/bin
default even when state.json explicitly recorded a custom bin_dir
(e.g. on Alpine and minimal Docker images that ship without jq by
default).

Fix: change `\(^["]*\)` → `\([^"]*\)` (negated character class
matching any non-quote character).  This is the standard "capture
the JSON string body up to the next `"`" pattern.

New bats test `update_preferred_user_bin_dir: parses bin_dir from
state without jq` constructs a state.json containing a
custom-bin path, isolates PATH to a fake bin directory containing
only sed and head (so jq is genuinely unavailable), and asserts
that the function returns the custom path rather than the default.
This nails the regression to the wall — if someone reverts the
regex fix, the test fails immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -238,7 +238,7 @@ update_preferred_user_bin_dir() {
         if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
             bin_dir="$(jq -r '.bin_dir // empty' "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
         else
-            bin_dir="$(sed -n 's/.*"bin_dir"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\(^["]*\)".*/\1/p' "$state_file" | head -n 1)"
+            bin_dir="$(sed -n 's/.*"bin_dir"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' "$state_file" | head -n 1)"
         fi
 
         if [[ -n "$bin_dir" ]] && [[ "$bin_dir" == /* ]] && [[ "$bin_dir" != "/" ]]; then

@@ -209,6 +209,38 @@ EOF
     assert_output "$target_home/.local/bin"
 }
 
+@test "update_preferred_user_bin_dir: parses bin_dir from state without jq" {
+    local current_home
+    local target_home
+    local state_file
+    local fake_path
+    local original_path="${PATH-}"
+    current_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    target_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    state_file="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/update-state.json"
+    fake_path="$(create_temp_dir)"
+
+    cat > "$state_file" <<EOF
+{"bin_dir":"$target_home/custom-bin"}
+EOF
+
+    ln -s /usr/bin/sed "$fake_path/sed"
+    ln -s /usr/bin/head "$fake_path/head"
+
+    export HOME="$current_home"
+    export PATH="$fake_path"
+    export TARGET_USER="ubuntu"
+    export TARGET_HOME="$target_home"
+    export ACFS_STATE_FILE="$state_file"
+    unset ACFS_BIN_DIR
+    unset ACFS_HOME
+
+    run update_preferred_user_bin_dir
+    PATH="${original_path:-/usr/bin:/bin}"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "$target_home/custom-bin"
+}
+
 @test "update_preferred_user_bin_dir: does not fall back to current HOME for different unresolved target" {
     local current_home
     current_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
